summary: iOS 🟥 Incorrect name and inaccessible settings after deleting device
scenario: Deleting a disconnected device from the Poly Studio Mobile app causes incorrect device name and connection status for remaining device, blocking access to settings
description_clean: After pairing at least two devices with the app, turning off one device, selecting the disconnected device, and deleting it from the app, the user is returned to the device home screen for the remaining device. However, the name and connection status shown are incorrect (they reflect the deleted device instead of the remaining device), and the user is unable to open settings for the remaining device. Restarting the app fixes the issue. Expected behavior: Deleting a device from the app should not cause any issues for devices that remain.
steps: 1. Open the Poly Studio Mobile app on your Android device.
2. Pair at least two Bluetooth audio devices (e.g., Device A and Device B) with the app.
3. From the Home screen, turn off one of the paired devices (e.g., Device A).
4. On the Home screen, select the disconnected device (Device A).
5. Delete the disconnected device (Device A) from the app.
6. Observe the Home screen, which now shows the remaining device (Device B).
7. Check the name and connection status displayed for the remaining device (Device B).
8. Attempt to open the Device Settings for the remaining device (Device B) by tapping on it.
expected: After deleting a device, the Home screen should correctly display the name and connection status of the remaining device. The user should be able to open Device Settings for the remaining device without any issues.
actual: The Home screen shows the name and connection status of the deleted device instead of the remaining device. The user is unable to open settings for the remaining device. Restarting the app resolves the issue.
